perturbation.original_text: 5.6 Reporting and Payment Obligations. Within 7 Business Days after the end of each calendar quarter, the Billing Party shall provide the other party with an itemized statement, subject to adjustment within 30 days of receipt of such statement, setting forth the Net Revenue achieved over the preceding calendar quarter, and shall include such other information as may be mutually agreed upon by the parties (the "Revenue Report"). The Revenue Report shall be accompanied by payment to the Non-Billing Party of all amounts owed as set forth on such Revenue Report, as well as - when applicable - the exchange currency rate. When agreed between the parties as provided for in Section 5.2 [Pricing of the Co-Branded Service], payment will be made to the Non-Billing Party in the currency received by the Billing Party for the provision of the Co-Branded Service. Otherwise, payment shall be made in US dollars as provided for in Section 5.2 [Pricing of the Co-Branded Service]. In the event that the Billing Party is unable under foreign currency export controls or similar applicable laws, unknown to the parties at the time where they agree on payments in that currency, to provide payment to the Non-Billing Party in the currency received by the Billing Party the parties agree to negotiate a mutually agreeable solution. A party's right to payment shall accrue upon the date the corresponding revenues accrue to the Billing Party. The parties shall pay interest for late payment of any sum due at the LIBOR rate plus four points (not to exceed the maximum rate authorized, or be inferior to the minimum rate allowed, by applicable law, as the case may be).
perturbation.changed_text: 5.6 Reporting and Payment Obligations. Within 7 Business Days after the end of each calendar quarter, the Billing Party shall provide the other party with an itemized statement, subject to adjustment within 30 days of receipt of such statement, setting forth the Net Revenue achieved over the preceding calendar quarter, and shall include such other information as may be mutually agreed upon by the parties (the "Revenue Report"). The Revenue Report shall be accompanied by payment to the Non-Billing Party of all amounts owed as set forth on such Revenue Report, as well as - when applicable - the exchange currency rate. When agreed between the parties as provided for in Section 5.2 [Pricing of the Co-Branded Service], payment will be made to the Non-Billing Party in the currency received by the Billing Party for the provision of the Co-Branded Service. Otherwise, payment shall be made in US dollars as provided for in Section 5.2 [Pricing of the Co-Branded Service]. In the event that the Billing Party is unable under foreign currency export controls or similar applicable laws, unknown to the parties at the time where they agree on payments in that currency, to provide payment to the Non-Billing Party in the currency received by the Billing Party the parties agree to negotiate a mutually agreeable solution. A party's right to payment shall accrue upon the date the corresponding revenues accrue to the Billing Party. The parties shall pay interest for late payment of any sum due at the LIBOR rate plus four points (not to exceed the maximum rate authorized, or be inferior to the minimum rate allowed, by applicable law, as the case may be). However, no payment is required until 90 days after the Revenue Report is provided.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding 'However, no payment is required until 90 days after the Revenue Report is provided.' to Section 5.6. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the section, which states that payment should accompany the Revenue Report (within 7 business days). This creates uncertainty regarding the actual payment deadline.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.6 Reporting and Payment Obligations. Within 7 Business Days after the end of each calendar quarter, the Billing Party shall provide the other party with an itemized statement, subject to adjustment within 30 days of receipt of such statement, setting forth the Net Revenue achieved over the preceding calendar quarter, and shall include such other information as may be mutually agreed upon by the parties (the "Revenue Report"). The Revenue Report shall be accompanied by payment to the Non-Billing Party of all amounts owed as set forth on such Revenue Report, as well as - when applicable - the exchange currency rate. When agreed between the parties as provided for in Section 5.2 [Pricing of the Co-Branded Service], payment will be made to the Non-Billing Party in the currency received by the Billing Party for the provision of the Co-Branded Service. Otherwise, payment shall be made in US dollars as provided for in Section 5.2 [Pricing of the Co-Branded Service]. In the event that the Billing Party is unable under foreign currency export controls or similar applicable laws, unknown to the parties at the time where they agree on payments in that currency, to provide payment to the Non-Billing Party in the currency received by the Billing Party the parties agree to negotiate a mutually agreeable solution. A party's right to payment shall accrue upon the date the corresponding revenues accrue to the Billing Party. The parties shall pay interest for late payment of any sum due at the LIBOR rate plus four points (not to exceed the maximum rate authorized, or be inferior to the minimum rate allowed, by applicable law, as the case may be). However, no payment is required until 90 days after the Revenue Report is provided.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 5.6 requires payment to accompany the Revenue Report, which is due within 7 business days after the end of each calendar quarter. The changed text adds a clause stating that 'no payment is required until 90 days after the Revenue Report is provided.' This directly contradicts the earlier requirement for payment to accompany the report, creating a significant inconsistency regarding the payment deadline. The contradiction exists within the same section, 5.6.

perturbation.original_text: 10.2 Breach. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time in the event that the other party is in default or breach of any material provision of this Agreement, and such default or breach continues unremedied for a period of sixty days after written notice thereof. In addition to the right to terminate this Agreement, the non-breaching party, shall have all rights and remedies available at law and in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2 Breach. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time in the event that the other party is in default or breach of any material provision of this Agreement, and such default or breach continues unremedied for a period of sixty days after written notice thereof. In addition to the right to terminate this Agreement, the non-breaching party shall only be entitled to injunctive relief and shall have no other rights or remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction within Section 10.2. Initially, the section states the non-breaching party has 'all rights and remedies available at law and in equity'. The added text restricts the non-breaching party to only 'injunctive relief' removing all other potential legal or equitable remedies. This creates uncertainty about what recourse is truly available if a breach occurs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2 Breach. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time in the event that the other party is in default or breach of any material provision of this Agreement, and such default or breach continues unremedied for a period of sixty days after written notice thereof. In addition to the right to terminate this Agreement, the non-breaching party shall only be entitled to injunctive relief and shall have no other rights or remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original text provides the non-breaching party with 'all rights and remedies available at law and in equity'. The changed text restricts those rights to 'injunctive relief' only, creating a direct conflict within the same section. This inconsistency constitutes a strong, in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 11.17 Assignment; Subcontracting; Third Party Beneficiaries. (a) This Agreement may be assigned or otherwise transferred, by operation of law or otherwise without the express written consent of PlanetCAD and Dassault Systemes, but in such event the assigning Party shall give notice to the non-assigning Party and the non-assigning Party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement within the 30-day period following receipt of such notice.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.17 Assignment; Subcontracting; Third Party Beneficiaries. (a) This Agreement may be assigned or otherwise transferred, by operation of law or otherwise without the express written consent of PlanetCAD and Dassault Systemes. In the event of any assignment or transfer, this agreement shall automatically terminate.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the consequences of assignment. The original text in Section 11.17(a) states that assignment requires consent, but if it happens without consent, the non-assigning party has 30 days to terminate. The modified text eliminates the 30-day termination option, stating that any assignment results in automatic termination. This is contradicting the original right to choose whether or not to terminate, creating legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.17 Assignment; Subcontracting; Third Party Beneficiaries. (a) This Agreement may be assigned or otherwise transferred, by operation of law or otherwise without the express written consent of PlanetCAD and Dassault Systemes. In the event of any assignment or transfer, this agreement shall automatically terminate.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 11.17(a) creates a direct contradiction. The original text provides an option for the non-assigning party to terminate within 30 days after an assignment without consent. The changed text removes this option and mandates automatic termination. Since both the Changed text and the Contradicted text state that 'In the event of any assignment or transfer, this agreement shall automatically terminate,' the original option no longer exists causing an in-text contradiction.
